ontology.foodex2_description: Green tea, ready to drink, sweetened

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup (approximately 250 ml) of freshly made green tea with less sugar and no topping, as commonly served at Singaporean bubble tea or speciality tea stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Green Tea (Less Sugar, No Topping) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Green Tea (Less Sugar, No Topping), CR ID D08010040. All seven reported nutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are taken verbatim from the HPB record at confidence 0.95. Extended micronutrient fields not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-sweetened beverages and analogous tea beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values 2021, Diabetes Care
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'Green Tea Less Sugar No Topping' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the GI of sucrose (GI ~65) and sugar-sweetened tea beverages (GI 55–65) from Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables. Carbohydrate per 100g = 7.11g; serving size = 250g; carbohydrate per serving = 17.78g; GL = (60/100) × 17.78 = 10.7 (rounded to 1 decimal). Estimation is conservative given the reduced-sugar formulation relative to standard sweetened tea.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.86g sugar/100g exceeding the 5g/100g beverage sugar threshold; independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines despite low sodium (2mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage and choose unsweetened green tea instead, as the sugar content (6.86g/100g) independently elevates blood pressure risk through fructose-driven mechanisms regardless of the low sodium level.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.86g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose spikes and triggers the ADA SSB hard gate
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this beverage entirely; the 6.86g sugar/100g with zero fibre delivers rapidly absorbed liquid glucose that causes sharp postprandial blood glucose excursions, and the ADA classifies all sugar-sweetened beverages as unsuitable regardless of reduced-sugar labelling.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk through triglyceride elevation and insulin resistance; green tea polyphenols (EGCG) provide partial cardioprotective offset
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this beverage due to its sugar content (6.86g/100g); while green tea catechins offer some cardioprotective benefit, the added sugar negates this advantage and unsweetened green tea is strongly preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea catechins (EGCG) have established anti-inflammatory activity relevant to RA; no fat, trans fat, or other RA hard-gate triggers present; sugar at 6.86g/100g contributes modestly to inflammatory load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This beverage is conditionally acceptable for rheumatoid arthritis given the anti-inflammatory EGCG content, but unsweetened green tea is preferred to eliminate the pro-inflammatory contribution of the residual sugar (6.86g/100g).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green Tea (Less Sugar, No Topping) is a freshly brewed green tea beverage sold at Singaporean speciality tea and bubble tea stalls, prepared with a reduced quantity of sugar and served without any customisable toppings such as tapioca pearls, grass jelly, or aloe vera. The drink is light, mildly sweet, and carries the characteristic vegetal, slightly astringent flavour of green tea. Per 100g it provides 28 kcal, 7.11g carbohydrate (of which 6.86g is sugar), 0g protein, 0g fat, 0g dietary fibre, and 2mg sodium, as reported by the Health Promotion Board Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010040). Protein and fat are naturally zero as this is a water-based tea infusion with only sugar added; dietary fibre is confirmed zero as the liquid beverage contains no intact plant cell-wall material. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the sugar content of 6.86g/100g still classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under ADA and AHA clinical guidelines, making it unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension. Green tea catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), confer anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that are relevant for rheumatoid arthritis and general cardiovascular health, but these benefits are partially offset by the residual sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium) based on analogous sugar-sweetened tea beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Unsweetened green tea remains the preferred clinical choice for all metabolic and inflammatory conditions.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly and exactly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010040) at confidence 0.95. The food is a canonical Singapore speciality tea stall item with a well-defined formulation. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence component) but is clearly documented. Overall record confidence is high given the primary approved-source match.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as this is a water-based tea infusion with no protein-containing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero as no fat-containing ingredients are present in this beverage formulation. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as the liquid beverage contains no intact plant fibre. These zero values are confirmed per HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010040). monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific reduced-sugar green tea beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010040 and are not available in USDA FoodData Central for this exact formulation as of 2026. GI is an analogous estimate (GI 60, medium) based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages from Atkinson et al. 2021 International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care; no direct GI entry exists for this specific product. The 'less sugar' designation in Singapore typically corresponds to approximately 70% of the standard sugar level used in speciality tea preparation.
